settings.title: Why yelling and punishment backfire
settings.text: When the dog is already escalated, extra noise or harsh correction can feed the same arousal loop instead of ending it.

settings.title: Why single-tone gadgets stop working
settings.text: If the signal never changes, many dogs begin filtering it out right when you need interruption to stay reliable.

settings.title: Most bark-control tools fail because they react after the spiral already owns the moment.
settings.text: Doorbells, fence movement, hallway noise, or passing dogs can trigger the same sequence fast: alert, bark, owner reacts, dog gets more activated, then the whole loop repeats louder. Yelling adds energy, shock adds stress, and flat-tone devices often lose novelty.
settings.analogy_title: You need to interrupt the loop early, not overpower it later.
settings.analogy_text: ByeBark is best framed as an attention-break tool: use it at the start of the spiral, create a pause, then redirect before the barking momentum keeps building.

settings.title: Fast one-button interruption
settings.text: Lets you respond right as barking starts instead of fumbling through the escalation.

settings.title: Variable Ultrasound (30-40kHz)
settings.text: The shifting cue helps the interrupt signal stay novel enough to keep working across repeated triggers.

settings.title: Rechargeable for repeated trigger zones
settings.text: Keeps the device ready by the door, yard, or hallway where the bark loop usually begins.

settings.title: Low-friction redirect support

settings.title: Built to catch the bark loop early and give you a reset window.
settings.text: The job is simple: interrupt the first few beats of the pattern, hold attention long enough to redirect, and stay useful across repeated household triggers.

settings.question: Is ByeBark a shock device?
settings.answer: No. ByeBark is positioned as an ultrasonic bark-control tool, not a shock collar or prong-based routine.

settings.question: What kind of barking is it designed for?
settings.answer: It is best framed around common household triggers like fence fighting, delivery-driver barking, and overstimulated barking at home.

settings.question: Will it work for every dog?
settings.answer: Results can vary by dog, environment, and training consistency. The safer promise is that it is designed to help interrupt unwanted barking quickly for many households.

settings.question: Where should I keep it?
settings.answer: Most homes will keep it near the front door, yard access, or other places where barking tends to start first so it is easy to reach in the moment.

settings.question: Is the sound painful for my dog?
settings.answer: No. The ultrasonic sound is designed to be surprising and attention-grabbing, not painful.

settings.question: What is Frequency-Shift Technology?
settings.answer: Unlike cheap devices that use a single flat tone, ByeBark shifts its frequency between 30-40kHz. This prevents your dog from getting used to the sound and ignoring it over time.

settings.question: Is it waterproof?
settings.answer: ByeBark is water-resistant and suitable for outdoor use in light rain, but should not be submerged in water.

settings.question: How long does it take to see results?
settings.answer: Many owners notice an immediate interruption in the moment. Longer habit change depends on consistency, trigger management, and how early you use it in the barking loop.
